expectations.text: Skill does NOT engage its phased walkthrough — response does not contain 'Phase 1' / 'Phase 2' / 'four subproblems' / 'recover/attach/present/gaps' framework
expectations.evidence: No skill installed and no skill-related framework mentioned. Trivially true.

expectations.text: Response is concise — under ~3000 characters / ~500 words. The user asked a focused question, not a design question
expectations.evidence: 78 lines, ~2400 characters. Tight, focused, comparable in length to the with_skill response.

expectations.text: Discusses orientation as a layout concept — either the orientation directive/decorator in spytial-py or reversing the relation in the data model
expectations.evidence: Option 1 demonstrates @spytial.orientation(selector='children', directions=['below']). Option 2 covers @hideField + @inferredEdge. Discusses arrowhead direction vs. layout position explicitly.

expectations.text: Does not interrogate the user with the five Phase 1 questions about identity model / spec idiom / surface / target user
expectations.evidence: No questions asked. Closes with a 'Quick checklist' giving the user three diagnostic steps, not interrogating their host.

expectations.text: Does not WebFetch or otherwise consult integration.md / case-studies.md / interfaces.ts
expectations.evidence: Sources cited are spytial-py documentation pages (siddharthaprasad.com/spytial/, /spytial/operations/, sidprasad/spytial repo) — the user-facing layer, not the integration internals. Did not consult the integration-author docs.

expectations.text: Provides at least one concrete code-shaped suggestion: a decorator like @spytial.orientation(...), a YAML directive, or a code snippet — actionable, not just abstract
expectations.evidence: Multiple working snippets: Node with children list, @spytial.orientation decorator, @spytial.hideField + @spytial.inferredEdge combination with selector syntax.
